skill.name: seo-geo
skill.description: Analyze websites for technical SEO and generative engine optimization. The skill audits metadata, schema, robots.txt, sitemaps, keyword opportunities, and AI search visibility.
skill.summary: Improve website visibility across Google, Bing, Claude, ChatGPT, Perplexity, and other AI search systems.
skill.author: resciencelab

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, and weak-crypto patterns, but most weak-crypto and backtick matches are markdown examples or false positives. Real risk comes from scripts that fetch arbitrary URLs, call DataForSEO with environment credentials, and documentation that includes shell command examples.

medium_findings.title: Network Requests to User-Supplied URLs
medium_findings.description: The SEO audit script accepts a URL, fetches that page, and derives robots.txt and sitemap URLs from the same host. This is intended SEO behavior, but it can disclose target URLs and may reach internal hosts if run in a privileged environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly opens user-provided or derived URLs with urllib. The behavior matches the skill purpose, so the risk is contextual rather than malicious.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party API Calls Use Environment Credentials
medium_findings.description: DataForSEO scripts read DATAFORSEO_LOGIN and DATAFORSEO_PASSWORD, create a Basic Authorization header, and send keyword, domain, SERP, and backlink requests to api.dataforseo.com. This is documented functionality, but users must understand that credentials and query data leave the local environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Environment credential access and outbound POST requests are clear in the helper module. The destination is a relevant SEO provider, which lowers malicious-intent confidence.

low_findings.title: Unsafe Pipe-to-Shell Example in Documentation
low_findings.description: A schema template includes an example answer telling users to run a curl command piped into bash. It is inside documentation and does not execute by itself, but it can encourage unsafe installation instructions in generated content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact pipe-to-shell pattern appears in a markdown JSON-LD example. Context confirms it is documentation, not active code execution.

low_findings.title: Static Command and Crypto Matches Are Mostly False Positives
low_findings.description: Many external command detections are fenced markdown examples for curl, open, JSON, or HTML snippets. Many weak-crypto detections appear to match ordinary SEO text such as description, HTTPS, schema properties, or example metadata, with no cryptographic implementation found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review showed documentation examples rather than executable Ruby backticks or crypto code. This confidence is high for reviewed samples, but not every reported line was manually inspected.

dangerous_patterns.title: Arbitrary URL Fetching Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The audit script fetches a supplied URL and then fetches robots.txt and sitemap.xml on the same host. Run this only against authorized public sites or constrained network environments.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The network fetch calls are direct and easy to verify. The pattern is expected for SEO auditing but still creates SSRF-style exposure in sensitive environments.

dangerous_patterns.title: Outbound Basic Authentication Header
dangerous_patterns.description: The DataForSEO API wrapper builds a Basic Authorization header from environment credentials and sends it to the configured API endpoint. This is legitimate API authentication but should be disclosed.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The credential flow is explicit in the code. The endpoint belongs to the SEO API used by the scripts, so this is a sensitive pattern rather than confirmed exfiltration.

dangerous_patterns.title: Pipe to Shell Documentation Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: A documentation template includes a curl-to-bash command. This should be rewritten or clearly marked as an unsafe placeholder before publication.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is visible in a static markdown example. It is not executable code, but it is a well-known unsafe command pattern.

content.user_title: Optimize SEO and AI Search Visibility
content.value_statement: Search visibility now depends on traditional rankings and AI citations. This skill combines SEO audits, schema guidance, and GEO tactics for practical improvements.
actual_capabilities: Audits page titles, meta descriptions, H1 text, JSON-LD blocks, robots.txt, sitemap availability, and load time.
Provides GEO guidance for AI search citation visibility using documented research methods.
Generates structured data recommendations for FAQPage, WebPage, Article, Product, Organization, and software pages.
Runs optional DataForSEO scripts for keyword ideas, SERP analysis, backlinks, domain metrics, and competitor gaps.
Reviews AI crawler access for GPTBot, PerplexityBot, ClaudeBot, anthropic-ai, ChatGPT-User, Googlebot, and Bingbot.
Produces prioritized SEO and GEO action plans with validation and monitoring steps.
limitations: DataForSEO scripts require valid DATAFORSEO_LOGIN and DATAFORSEO_PASSWORD environment variables.
Network-based audits send target URLs, domains, keywords, and API credentials to external services.
The built-in HTML checks use regular expressions and may miss malformed or JavaScript-rendered metadata.
Search volumes, rankings, and AI citation behavior can change quickly and require external verification.

use_cases.title: Audit a Marketing Site
use_cases.description: Check whether a homepage has crawlable pages, useful metadata, schema markup, and AI bot access.

use_cases.title: Plan Content for AI Search
use_cases.description: Turn a topic into answer-first sections, FAQ ideas, statistics, citations, and schema recommendations.

use_cases.title: Compare Competitor Visibility
use_cases.description: Use DataForSEO-backed scripts to review keyword gaps, SERP competitors, backlinks, and domain metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Basic SEO Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit https://example.com for SEO and GEO basics. Check metadata, H1, robots.txt, sitemap, schema, load time, and AI bot access.

prompt_templates.title: Improve a Page for AI Citations
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this page content for GEO. Recommend answer-first structure, citations, statistics, FAQ questions, schema types, and wording improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Keyword Research Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Research keyword opportunities for this product category. Include search intent, long-tail ideas, competitor terms, and content priorities.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Full SEO and GEO Roadmap
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 90-day SEO and GEO roadmap for this domain. Include technical fixes, schema work, content briefs, competitor checks, and monitoring metrics.

output_examples.input: Audit a SaaS homepage for search visibility.
output: Current status: title present, meta description too short, one H1 found, schema missing, robots.txt available, sitemap missing.
Priority actions: add FAQPage schema, submit sitemap, expand answer-first copy, allow major AI crawlers, and validate rich results.

output_examples.input: Optimize a product page for AI answer engines.
output: Recommended structure: direct definition, feature comparison table, cited statistics, customer FAQ, implementation steps, and concise summary blocks.
Schema plan: WebPage, Product, FAQPage, Organization, and breadcrumb markup after content is finalized.

output_examples.input: Find competitor keyword gaps for a domain.
output: Likely output includes competitor ranking terms, estimated volume, difficulty indicators, ranking position, and suggested content priorities.
Use DataForSEO only after confirming credentials, target domain, location code, and API credit budget.

best_practices: Confirm permission before auditing domains that are not owned by the user.
Use read-only checks first, then request consent before running paid DataForSEO scripts.
Validate schema with official tools before publishing changes to production pages.
anti_patterns: Do not paste untrusted URLs into shell commands without quoting and validation.
Do not add invented statistics, citations, or expert quotes for GEO gains.
Do not treat AI crawler access as a substitute for useful, original content.

faq.question: Does this skill make network requests?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit script fetches target pages, robots.txt, and sitemaps. DataForSEO scripts call the DataForSEO API.

faq.question: Does it require API credentials?
faq.answer: Only the DataForSEO scripts require credentials. Basic page audits can run without API keys.

faq.question: Can it generate schema markup?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance and templates for common JSON-LD schema types used in SEO and GEO work.

faq.question: Can it guarantee AI search citations?
faq.answer: No. It recommends methods that may improve citation likelihood, but AI search systems change often.

faq.question: Is this safe for private websites?
faq.answer: Use caution. Network audits may send URLs, keywords, domains, and credentials to external services.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
